title: James Madison University Campus Tour
body: Class of '94.  I loved the campus back then, and it has only grown to be even more beautiful.  Practically lived in the Stacks at the Carrier Library, best quiet place to study back then.  The Quad was also a great place to hangout.
author: @cybrnathan

title: James Madison University Campus Tour
body: Loved the tour, this makes our short list for my 11th grader. We would like to see more of the Science/Engineering department.
author: @AsfordAryee

title: James Madison University Campus Tour
body: ('84) All of the cow tipping pastures are now gone. LOL.  What a campus (it's doubled in size since I was there) yet it still feels like JMU!  Still an amazing teaching university.
author: @DavidTangman

title: James Madison University Campus Tour
body: Beautiful campus unfortunately we picked a terrible time to try and visit  werent able to go in or see anything. No students or anyone on campus so just had to stroll around and look into windows....so disappointing.

title: James Madison University Campus Tour
body:
I appreciate this tour so much! The programs and community seem to have such a unique spirit about that I feel connected to even in this video!
How far is East Campus from Main Campus, and are there options for students to travel from campus to campus (i.e buses or shuttles) or just walking?
author: @eleanorjones8613

title: James Madison University Campus Tour
body:
I was really apprehensive at first with James Madison, steering towards Virginia Tech, because of the STEM opportunities. But this really really just made me change my mind. There seem to be dozens more here just for living life, where VT slightly stresses m out with only focusing on its academic advantages. The video did kinda disappoint me though in failing to point out the comp sci building. I'm going to have to assume it's in the engineering building lol.

How far away is the east campus from the original campus, too? Because it comparison that area seemed much blander and void of life compared to the rest of it.
author: @ChaseDetrick

author: @SB-cs2yv

title: Aerial Tour of JMU's Campus
body:
Damn, it looks like you guys redesigned maybe a third of the campus since I graduated 15 years ago. How much money did you waste unnecessarily demolishing and recreating buildings to make them look just a little shinier?

I wish that the students took seriously the "Harrisonburg has a lot to do" idea but sadly a lot of them just stick to the housing communities around campus and never venture to the city of anything. Hopefully, it's a little bit of a bubble since I was there
author: @okonh0wp

title: TOP 10 REASONS FOR ATTENDING JAMES MADISON UNIVERSITY (JMU) // katie elizabeth
body: JMU is my alma mater, it's the best school in VA. The best way I've ever heard the university and it's student body described was by Dr. Bolyard in the philosophy department. He said JMU kids are smart enough to go UVA or William & Mary but they're just a little to lazy to study on the weekends and they love to party. That pretty much sums of the culture, put in work Sunday afternoon - Thursday afternoon but from about 8 pm Thursday to 5 am Sunday morning drink and drink and drink. The academic load probably won't let you party on a Tuesday, though.
author: @benjamindouglas862

title: day in the life as a line cook at a three star Michelin restaurant #dayinthelife #cook #nyc
body: The restaurant business is BRUTAL.. you work your ass off for not much money… I just hope his love of cooking makes up for it..
author: @Madmaxx0829

title: day in the life as a line cook at a three star Michelin restaurant #dayinthelife #cook #nyc
body: Pre Covid I worked L’atelier Joel robuchon in nyc as a barback. I even developed a few cocktails on the menu. During the lockdown I landed a job in tech but I still dream of working in a kitchen again like that. Funnest job I’ve ever had. Keep it going!
author: @jonfouquier

title: day in the life as a line cook at a three star Michelin restaurant #dayinthelife #cook #nyc
body: I ate here last year. It was incredibly fancy, but just wasn't for me. It was right around $300 per person and they brought you multiple plates over a couple hour span. The food wasn't really anything bizarre. It was mostly all minimalist plates made with normal everyday ingredients, just cooked and stylized incredibly well. When I say minimalist plates I mean like a single piece of asparagus on a plate with a tiny half teaspoon of sauce on the side. I showed up hungry and left several hours later even hungrier. It was an interesting experience, but was it worth the price? Fuuuuuuuuuuuuck no. This is a place for rich people to take their rich friends to impress each other and nothing more.
author: @bigdeuce66

title: day in the life as a line cook at a three star Michelin restaurant #dayinthelife #cook #nyc
body: I was a culinary student back in 1998. I tried it for a year, but then switched over to the front of the house. I was more successful at FOH than BOH; it was a rough life because I attended college while working. I graduated in 2009 at the age of 33. Better late than never. I am grateful for being able to put myself through school through working in restaurants and catering.
author: @joshuacooper1899

title: day in the life as a line cook at a three star Michelin restaurant #dayinthelife #cook #nyc
